title: thlaspi caerulescens subsp. occitanicum
intro: Thlaspi caerulescens subsp. occitanicum, a subspecies of the alpine pennycress plant, is a type of flowering plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its high concentration of glucosinolates, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and supporting the immune system. Thlaspi caerulescens subsp. occitanicum is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
